title: Battle of Vanquo
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Battle |prev=[[Ambush on Suurja]][[Knights of the Old Republic 6|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 6]] |conc= |next=[[Taris Siege]]{{KOTORbackups|13}} |image=[[File:Mandalorians_invade_Vanquo.jpg]] |name=Battle of Vanquo |conflict=[[Mandalorian Wars]][[Knights of the Old Republic 7|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 7]] |date=[[3964 BBY]] |place=[[Vanquo]] |result=[[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] victory''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' |side1=[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] |side2=[[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] |side3= |side4= |commanders1=*[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Noma Sommos]][[Knights of the Old Republic 8|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 8]] *Admiral [[Jimas Veltraa]] *[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Saul Karath]] |commanders2=*[[Mandalore the Ultimate]] *[[Cassus Fett]] *[[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Rohlan Dyre]] |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |forces1=*[[Aurek-class tactical strikefighter|''Aurek''-class tactical strikefighters]] *[[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruisers]] *1 [[Inexpugnable-class tactical command ship|''Inexpugnable''-class tactical command ship]]''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' **[[Courageous (Inexpugnable-class)|''Courageous'']] |forces2=*[[Mandalorian shock trooper]]s *[[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droids]] *[[Davaab-type starfighter|''Davaab''-type starfighters]] *[[Jehavey'ir-type assault ship|''Jehavey'ir''-type assault ships]] *[[Kyramud-type battleship|''Kyramud''-type battleships]] *[[Meteor-class Q-Carrier|''Meteor''-class Q-Carriers]] *[[Speeder bike/Legends|Scout speeders]] *[[Shaadlar-type troopship|''Shaadlar''-type troopships]] *[[Teroch-type gunship|''Teroch''-type gunships]] |forces3= |forces4= |casual1= |casual2= |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|Blast it! I hate it when my cons come true.|[[Marn Hierogryph]]|}} The '''Battle of Vanquo''' took place in [[3964 BBY]], when the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] attacked the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Mining/Legends|mining]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Vanquo]] at the beginning of their [[Mandalorian Wars|war]] with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. A resource world near the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]]' true target—the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|ecumenopolis]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]—Vanquo was part of a Republic security cordon known as the [[Jebble-Vanquo-Tarnith line]] designed to protect Taris from Mandalorian aggression. That did not deter the Mandalorians from attacking Vanquo as part of a three-pronged offensive aimed at Taris, with Neo-Crusader leader [[Mandalore the Ultimate]] designing the Vanquo battle to lure away the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy's]] left flank and open up a corridor to Taris. [[Squad/Legends|Squads]] of [[Mandalorian shock trooper]]s easily secured the mining camps on Vanquo's nightside, while the Mandalorian and Republic [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]] engaged in a heated [[Space warfare/Legends|space battle]] above the planet. Eventually, Republic [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Saul Karath]] was forced to retreat, and the Mandalorians conquered the planet. The Mandalorian victory at Vanquo gave the Neo-Crusaders an Outer Rim stronghold they never relinquished and also set the stage for their [[Taris Siege|conquest]] of Taris not long afterward. ==Prelude== {{Quote|I'll tell ya, if the Mandalorians ever move on Vanquo, they'll find nothing here but people they've already seen!|A Vanquo miner, regarding the refugee situation on Vanquo|}} In [[3976 BBY]], the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior clans, at the command of leader [[Mandalore the Ultimate]], began to carve out an empire by conquering unaligned [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]], drawing no action from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. After amassing a sizable amount of territory, the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] lurched out toward the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]], encroaching on Republic [[Realspace/Legends|space]] and threatening resource worlds near the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|ecumenopolis]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. One of the worlds in [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore's]] sight was [[Vanquo]], a rugged [[Mining/Legends|mining]] planet that had become a destination for [[Refugee/Legends|refugees]] from the Mandalorian crusades. With the Mandalorian threat looming, Vanquo became part of a Republic security cordon known as the [[Jebble-Vanquo-Tarnith line]]. Designed to defend Taris, the line was host to heavy, stalemated fighting on worlds like [[Suurja]]{{KOTORbackups|0}}—however, in [[3964 BBY]], all this changed. At Taris' [[Jedi Tower]], four of the five [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] undergoing [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] as the next generation of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] on the planet were [[Padawan Massacre|murdered]], supposedly by the lone surviving classmate, [[Zayne Carrick]]. After Carrick escaped pursuit and fled the planet, public confidence in the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] was severely shaken, leading to the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] withdrawing the five [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] stationed at the Jedi Tower back to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Mandalore the Ultimate took this as a signal to begin [[Gender/Legends|his]] attack, launching a three-pronged offensive aimed at seizing Taris. The real [[Mandalorian Wars]] had begun. ==The battle== {{Quote|It's working, ''Mand'alor''. The Republic fleet's left flank has split to cover ''Vanquo''—clearing the way for our main thrust!''"
"''Then the path to ''Taris'' is clear, at last.|Cassus Fett and Mandalore the Ultimate discuss their attack on Vanquo|}} [[File:Battle_of_Vanquo.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The space battle above Vanquo]] Mandalore intended all along for the attack on Vanquo to be a diversion, meant to draw the Republic [[Fleet/Legends|fleet's]] left flank and open the way for the Mandalorians' main thrust toward Taris. To that end, the Mandalorians landed in force on the nightside of the planet, led by [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Rohlan Dyre]], who had been in trouble for desertion on several previous occasions. The [[Mandalorian shock trooper|shock infantry]] touched down among the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] camps that dotted Vanquo's [[Mountain/Legends|mountainous]] landscape, many dropping down on [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droids]] and [[Speeder bike/Legends|scout speeders]] as well as [[Meteor-class Q-Carrier|''Meteor''-class Q-Carriers]] and [[Shaadlar-type troopship|''Shaadlar''-type troopships]]—at one [[Unidentified mining outpost|outpost]], a Mandalorian force, including Dyre himself, happened across the fugitive Zayne Carrick and his friends, who had previously faked a Mandalorian attack on the outpost to clear it for scavenging. The group was caught by surprise, and the [[Arkanian Offshoot/Legends|Arkanian Offshoot]] [[Jarael]], who had been posing as a Jedi in their previous con, was taken [[Prison/Legends|prisoner]] on a [[Teroch-type gunship|''Teroch''-type gunship]]. As the others attempted to escape, Dyre used the commotion as a cover to escape the battle in the group's [[Starship/Legends|ship]], [[The Last Resort (starship)|''The Last Resort'']]. Carrick's group caught up to Dyre as he was flying toward [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], with ''The Last Resort'' under heavy Mandalorian [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] fire. However, Dyre succeeded in flying ''The Last Resort'' out of the Vanquo battle zone. Otherwise, the battle went off without a hitch for the Mandalorians. The shock [[Troop/Legends|troops]] encountered minimal resistance at the mining camps, and Mandalore's [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]], [[Cassus Fett]], reported that an orbital screen was being set up over the dayside of the planet so the major settlements there could be engaged—also, the Mandalorians had succeeded in drawing the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy's]] flank away. Meanwhile, an intense [[Space warfare/Legends|space battle]] raged over Vanquo, as dozens of Mandalorian [[Kyramud-type battleship|''Kyramud''-type battleships]] clashed with Republic [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruisers]], with the Hammerheads launching [[Flight group/Legends|flights]] of [[Aurek-class tactical strikefighter|''Aurek''-class tactical strikefighters]] that flew against [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] of Mandalorian [[Davaab-type starfighter|''Davaab'' starfighters]]. The Republic forces were led by [[Admiral/Legends|Admirals]] [[Jimas Veltraa]] and [[Noma Sommos]], who were coordinating the defense against the entirely of the Mandalorian attack toward Taris. At Vanquo itself, the Republic defense fleet was helmed by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Saul Karath]] in his [[Inexpugnable-class tactical command ship|''Inexpugnable''-class tactical command ship]], the [[Courageous (Inexpugnable-class)|''Courageous'']], which held the center of the Republic line. Karath defended Vanquo with all his strength but eventually had to fall back and retreat, allowing the Mandalorians to take the planet. ==Aftermath== Not only did the Mandalorian conquest of Vanquo give the Neo-Crusaders a stronghold on the Rim that they would not relinquish, but the battle also helped set the stage for the Mandalorians' [[Taris Siege|attack]] on Taris shortly afterward.{{CSWECite|III|294|Vanquo}} The Jebble-Vanquo-Tarnith line broken, Republic Admiral Jimas Veltraa reformed the remnants of the Outer Rim fleets into a line to defend Taris. However, the Mandalorians still captured Taris, with Veltraa [[Death/Legends|perishing]] in the destruction of his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Reliance (Mandalorian Wars)|''Reliance'']]. Saul Karath's strong defense of and fighting retreat from Vanquo proved to be a career-maker for the captain, as he was promoted to admiral after the battle was over. ==Behind the scenes== The Battle of Vanquo first appeared in [[Knights of the Old Republic 7|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 7]], a [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] written by [[John Jackson Miller]] and released in [[2006]]. The action continued in the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|series]]' [[Knights of the Old Republic 8|next issue]], and the result of the battle, along with further information about the event, were given in the supplements ''[[The Admiral's List: Jimas Veltraa Memorial Edition]]'' and the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''. The battle was first named the "Battle of Vanquo" in the [[2008]] ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of the Battle of Vanquo}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 7|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 7]] {{1st}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 8|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 8]] *{{KOTORbackups|13}} {{Mo}} *{{KOTORbackups|22}} {{Mo}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 49|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 49]] {{Imo}} ==Sources== *''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' {{1stID|Battle of Vanquo}} *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *{{SW|url=news/the-history-of-mandalorian-armor|text=The History of Mandalorian Armor|int=The History of Mandalorian Armor|nolive=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{MandalorianWarsNav}} {{Interlang |de=Schlacht von Vanquo |es=Batalla de Vanquo |it=Battaglia di Vanquo |pl=Bitwa o Vanquo }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Vanquo, Battle of}} [[Category:Battles of the Mandalorian Wars]] [[Category:Battles of Vanquo]]
